data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Powell Discusses U.S. Economic Stability
data.description:
Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell addressed the public on January 29, stating that the U.S. economy remains fundamentally strong. According to BlockBeats, Powell noted that the economy expanded at a steady pace last year. He also mentioned signs of stabilization in the unemployment rate.

Powell emphasized that the current policy stance is appropriate. He acknowledged that the recent government shutdown likely impacted economic growth in the fourth quarter, but he expects this effect to be reversed.

data.title: UK Watchdog Bans Coinbase Ads for Linking Crypto to Cost-of-Living Crisis
data.description:
The UK’s advertising regulator has banned a high-profile marketing campaign by Coinbase, ruling that the ads irresponsibly implied cryptocurrency could help address the country’s cost-of-living pressures. The Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) concluded that the campaign breached social responsibility rules by trivializing financial risk at a time of widespread economic strain.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/uk-watchdog-bans-coinbase-ads-for-linking-crypto-to-cost-of-living-crisis/

data.title: HOT MOMENTS: Gold Surpasses $5,300 – What’s Going On? Here Are Analysts’ Expectations
data.description:
Gold, which has been breaking records in recent days, climbed to a new all-time high in dollar terms. Continue Reading: HOT MOMENTS: Gold Surpasses $5,300 – What’s Going On? Here Are Analysts’ Expectations
source: https://en.bitcoinsistemi.com/hot-moments-gold-surpasses-5-300-what-s-going-on-here-are-analysts-expectations/

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: The U.S. economy is on solid foundations, and current policies are helping to make progress toward achieving our two main goals.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell stated that the U.S. economy has a solid foundation, having expanded at a steady pace last year, and will enter 2026 on a solid basis. The unemployment rate has shown some signs of stabilizing. Current policies are helping to make progress towards achieving the two main goals. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: Policy rates are within a reasonable range of estimates of the neutral rate.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell: Policy rates are within a reasonable range of estimates of the neutral rate. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: The Fed is unlikely to cut interest rates before Powell leaves office; Waller is trying to get Trump's attention.
data.description: Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at Carson Group, said the Fed didn't make any waves, and the market widely expected them to press the pause button. We probably won't see any rate cuts until Powell leaves the Fed in May. The potential good news is that they mentioned some positive factors regarding the labor market, but inflation is clearly still a concern. We know Milan entered the Fed to stir things up, but Waller's choice is somewhat intriguing. His name is still on the list of potential next chair candidates, so I'm sure he's also trying to get Trump's attention to show he remains firmly in the dovish camp. The Fed's independence is indeed a real issue, and the next chair will face enormous pressure to cut rates. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Chairmanship Speculations Amid Waller's Opposition Vote
data.description:
Currently, Christopher Waller's dissenting vote is being linked to his potential candidacy for the Federal Reserve Chair position. According to Odaily, Pepperstone analyst Michael Brown suggests that this might keep Waller's slim chances of succeeding Chairman Jerome Powell alive for now.

Polymarket data indicates that Kevin Warsh has a 28% probability of being elected as the Federal Reserve Chair, Rick Rieder's chances have risen to 39%, Kevin Hassett's probability stands at 6%, and Christopher Waller's chances are at 12%.

data.title: Former Fed Vice Chair Clarida Anticipates Powell to Avoid Dollar Discussion
data.description: On January 29, former Federal Reserve Vice Chair Richard Clarida expressed his expectations regarding Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's upcoming question-and-answer session. According to BlockBeats, Clarida stated that he does not anticipate Powell addressing the issue of the U.S. dollar. Clarida noted, "The Federal Reserve tends to steer clear of any and all discussions related to exchange rates."

data.title: Intel Shares Surge Following Positive Q4 Earnings and AI Demand Signals
data.description: Intel's stock experienced a significant rebound, climbing over 10% after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings. According to NS3.AI, the positive results were driven by encouraging signals of AI-driven semiconductor demand. Despite this, Intel's forecast for first-quarter revenue fell short of expectations, prompting mixed reactions from analysts. Peers such as Texas Instruments and ASML have contributed to renewed confidence in the market. Analysts have varied opinions, with price targets for Intel ranging from $25 to $39, highlighting ongoing concerns about market volatility.

data.title: Optimism Governance Approves OP Token Buyback Plan for Ecosystem Sustainability
data.description: Optimism's governance has approved a plan to buy back OP tokens, funded by revenue from the Superchain, to enhance ecosystem sustainability and align incentives across the network. According to BWEnews, this initiative aims to bolster the long-term viability of the Optimism ecosystem by strategically utilizing Superchain-generated funds. The decision reflects a commitment to maintaining a balanced and incentivized network structure, ensuring that stakeholders are adequately rewarded and motivated to contribute to the ecosystem's growth. The market capitalization of OP tokens currently stands at $1.231 billion, although this figure may be subject to change.

data.title: Federal Reserve Holds Interest Rates Steady, Precious Metals React Positively
data.description: The Federal Reserve has decided to keep its interest rates unchanged, aligning with market expectations. According to NS3.AI, this decision led to a brief increase in the prices of precious metals. Spot gold experienced a rise of $14, reaching $5290.75 per ounce, while spot silver saw an increase of $1.20, climbing to $112.42 per ounce. These fluctuations suggest a short-term positive response in the precious metals market following the Fed's announcement.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Expects the Federal Reserve to resume rate cuts later this year
data.description: Goldman Sachs analyst Kay Hay stated that given strong economic data and signs of stabilization in the labor market, the Federal Reserve is likely to maintain its current policy for the time being. However, we expect interest rate cuts to resume later this year, as slowing inflation will allow the Fed to implement two more "normalization" rate cuts, bringing interest rates back to what the Federal Open Market Committee members consider a neutral level. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: Waller's hawkish stance keeps his Fed chair campaign alive
data.description: Currently, Waller's dissenting votes are being linked to his candidacy for Federal Reserve Chair, at least according to Michael Brown, an analyst at Pepperston. He stated that this "might temporarily sustain Waller's slim hope of succeeding Powell as Chair." Polymarket data shows that Kevin Warsh's probability of being elected Fed Chair is currently 28%, Rick Rieder's has risen to 39%, Kevin Hassett's is currently 6%, and Christopher Waller's is currently 12%.

data.title: Analysts: Powell is expected to avoid discussing his post-retirement plans at the press conference.
data.description: Pepperston analyst Michael Brown said the policy statement was largely unchanged, although the assessment of economic conditions was revised upward to reflect a "solid" pace of growth. He said attention will turn to the press conference, where Federal Reserve Chairman Powell may mention that the current federal funds rate is within a reasonable range for the neutral rate, but he will likely firmly avoid any questions about what happens after May. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve May Adjust Inflation Measures, Says Schwab Strategist
data.description: Schwab's Chief Strategist Kathy Jones has indicated that the Federal Reserve's FOMC statement might include adjustments related to inflation. According to ChainCatcher, Jones noted that the current financial conditions are relatively loose. The potential changes in the FOMC statement could reflect the ongoing economic dynamics and the Fed's approach to managing inflation.

data.title: White House to Convene Industry Leaders to Discuss Senate Crypto Bill
data.description: The White House is set to host a meeting on Monday with executives from the banking and cryptocurrency sectors to address the stalled Senate crypto bill. According to BWEnews, this gathering aims to facilitate discussions on the legislative challenges and potential pathways forward for the bill. The meeting underscores the administration's commitment to engaging with industry stakeholders to navigate the complexities of cryptocurrency regulation. The Senate bill, which has faced delays, is a focal point of concern for both the financial and crypto industries, as it could significantly impact regulatory frameworks and market operations. The White House's initiative reflects an effort to bridge gaps and foster collaboration between traditional financial institutions and the burgeoning crypto sector.

data.title: FOMC Statement: Federal Reserve Governors Milan and Waller dissented from this interest rate decision and supported a 25 basis point rate cut.
data.description: The Federal Reserve FOMC statement: Federal Reserve Governors Milan and Waller dissented from this interest rate decision and supported a 25 basis point rate cut. (Jinshi)

data.title: The Federal Reserve held rates steady as expected, and spot gold and silver both rose in the short term.
data.description: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady as expected, causing spot gold to rise $14 to $5290.75 per ounce, while spot silver rose $1.20 to $112.42 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: FOMC Statement: The committee members voted 10-2 to approve the interest rate decision.
data.description: The Federal Reserve FOMC stated that the committee members voted 10-2 to approve the interest rate decision. (The vote at the previous meeting was 9-3.)

data.title: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged as expected, with Waller and Milan voting against it.
data.description: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged today, citing persistently high inflation and robust economic growth. The policy statement offered little clue as to when another rate cut might occur. The Fed decided to maintain the benchmark interest rate in the 3.50%–3.75% range by a 10-2 vote. The statement said, “Economic activity continues to expand at a solid pace.” Governors Waller and Milan voted against a 25-basis-point cut. The statement did not signal the timing of the next rate cut, only noting that the magnitude and timing of “further adjustments” would depend on subsequent data and the economic outlook. Meanwhile, the Fed stated that inflation “remains slightly high” and that the labor market “has shown some signs of stabilization.” Although the Fed noted that “job growth remains low,” it removed statements from its previous statements regarding increased downside risks to employment, indicating that policymakers’ overall concerns about a rapid decline in the labor market have eased. (Jinshi)
